# Related Content

The Related Content molecule renders an editor-set heading followed by a list of references to other site content. It is rendered by the `related_content_block` Drupal plugin, which embeds the `entity_reference_for_fields:embed_related_content` view and hands the molecule:

- a heading string (configurable per block placement, defaults to "Related Content")
- the rendered HTML output of the view, where each row shows the referenced content's type and a linked title

The molecule sits at the bottom of nodes that have a `field_related_content` reference list — typically Resources, but the block is reusable across any node bundle that adopts the field.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to experiment with the heading and the number of sample rows.

## Properties

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Molecule
- **Template**: `yds-related-content.twig`
- **Styles**: `_yds-related-content.scss`

### Markup contract

The view markup the molecule expects is the standard Views default style + fields row output:

```html
<div class="view view-entity-reference-for-fields ...">
  <div class="view-content">
    <div class="views-row">
      <div class="views-field views-field-type">
        <span class="field-content">Resource</span>
      </div>
      <div class="views-field views-field-title">
        <span class="field-content"><a href="/path">Title</a></span>
      </div>
    </div>
    ...
  </div>
</div>
```

`related_content__view` is rendered into the molecule with the `|raw` filter — the HTML is built upstream (by Drupal in production, by the Storybook fixture builder here) so escaping it would print the markup as text. This matches the pattern used by `yds-publication-detail`, `yds-image`, and `yds-video-embed`.

### Accessibility

- Heading is a semantic `<h2>` styled to h3 size to keep document outline correct underneath the page title.
- Section element provides a landmark a screen-reader user can skip to.
- Each row's content type is exposed as plain text above the linked title so the type is announced before the link.

## Usage Guidelines

### When to use

- Below the main content of any node that has a populated `field_related_content` list.
- Wherever you want a simple "see also" list of typed content references; this molecule does not render images or summaries.

### When not to use

- For curated promotional links — use Callouts.
- For lists of cards with images and summaries — use the Card Collection organism.

## Related Components

- **Card Collection**: For richer lists of cards with images, summaries, and grid layouts.
- **Callout**: For editor-curated promotional callouts with CTAs.
